creator: ports@grosbein.net
text:
Kris Kennaway wrote:
 
 > > >Fix:
 > >
 > >       bsd.port.mk should not prevent user from building/installing
 > >       a port when BROKEN contains the word 'Unfetchable' and
 > >       distfile is accessible localy.
 > >
 > >       Alternatively, use some other directive instead of BROKEN
 > >       to mark (possibly temporary) unfetchable ports and first
 > >       check distfile availability in CD_MOUNTPTS/DISTDIR.
 > 
 > You can just submit your fix to make the port accessible again,
 
 Hmm, its master site is out of my control
 and I doubt there may be a patch to revive a server in the Internet :-)
 
 > or build with TRYBROKEN=1 as a workaround.
 
 Thank you, I did not know about this knob.
 
 > Since this is an exceptional
 > condition that is *supposed* to be transient I don't think it needs
 > further exceptional logic to handle it.  Rather users should be
 > encouraged to correct the error permanently.
 
 I think that transient error with master site connectivity
 should not prevent users from installing the port when they
 have distfiles on CD/DVD and catched 'BROKEN' after they have updated ports
 tree.
 
 Eugene Grosbein.
